name: From La Puebla del Río to La Isla Mayor – circular via the Dehesa de Abajo Nature Reserve
name_original: Desde La Puebla del Río hasta La Isla Mayor – circular por la Reserva Natural Concertada Dehesa de Abajo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Puebla Del Río traverse a landscape shaped by the Guadalquivir River and its marshlands, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve, characterized by Mediterranean forests, wild olive groves, and lakes. Routes often follow the river's course, providing views of wetlands and grasslands. The area presents a mix of flat sections and gentle hills, with both paved and unpaved paths available. Best road cycling routes around La Puebla Del Río The most popular road cycling route is From La Puebla del Río to La Isla Mayor – circular via the Dehesa de Abajo Nature Reserve, a 30.1 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This route explores the diverse ecosystems of the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Fighter Jet Exhibit – San Juan Bridge loop from Coria del Río, an easy 16.4 miles (26.4 km) path. This route offers views along the Guadalquivir River and passes the San Juan Bridge. Local road cyclists also love the Roadbike loop from La Puebla del Río, a 19.7 miles (31.7 km) trail leading through varied local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Road cycling around La Puebla Del Río is defined by the Guadalquivir River, the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in La Puebla Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 20 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Puebla Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around La Puebla Del Río?
A: The road cycling routes in La Puebla Del Río offer a varied landscape. You'll find a mix of flat sections, particularly along the Guadalquivir River, and gentle hills as you explore areas like the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve. The region features both paved and unpaved paths, catering to different cycling preferences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, La Puebla Del Río offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31915825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fighter Jet Exhibit – San Juan Bridge loop from Coria del Río&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.4-mile (26.4 km) path that provides scenic views along the Guadalquivir River. Overall, 19 of the 43 available routes are rated as easy.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in La Puebla Del Río?
A: Road cycling routes often traverse the diverse ecosystems of the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve, featuring Mediterranean forests, wild olive groves, and lakes like La Rianzuela. You can also enjoy picturesque riverside views along the Guadalquivir River. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5324565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadaíra Riverside Walk&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1606627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt; is also a key point of interest.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in La Puebla Del Río are designed as loops. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996063847/from-la-puebla-del-rio-to-la-isla-mayor-circular-via-the-dehesa-de-abajo-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From La Puebla del Río to La Isla Mayor – circular via the Dehesa de Abajo Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 30.1-mile (48.4 km) trail exploring the natural reserve. Another local favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17227256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from La Puebla del Río&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.7-mile (31.7 km) trail through varied local landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in La Puebla Del Río?
A: The region's climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, with comfortable conditions for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable during those months.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in La Puebla Del Río?
A: The road cycling routes in La Puebla Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views along the Guadalquivir River, and the well-maintained paths within the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve.

Q: Are there any longer distance road cycling routes available near La Puebla Del Río?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31992463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Bridge – Super road for racing bikes loop from La Puebla del Río&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial challenge, covering 70 km (43.5 miles). Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21842466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Bridge – Church of Santa María loop from La Puebla del Río&lt;/a&gt;, which is 56.4 km (35 miles) long.

Q: Is La Puebla Del Río a good destination for family-friendly road cycling?
A: Yes, the area offers several easy routes that are suitable for families. The varied terrain includes many flat sections, especially along the river, making it accessible for different skill levels. The presence of natural reserves with designated paths also provides a safe and enjoyable environment for family rides.

Q: Are there facilities like cafes or places to rest along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for each route, the region is generally well-equipped for visitors. The Dehesa de Abajo visitor center serves as a starting point for various routes and offers facilities. You'll find amenities in La Puebla Del Río itself and in nearby towns, which can be incorporated into your cycling plans for breaks and refreshments.

Q: What is the total number of road cycling routes available in La Puebla Del Río?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes available in and around La Puebla Del Río, offering a wide range of options for different distances and difficulty levels. This includes 19 easy routes and 24 moderate routes.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a more challenging ride?
A: While the region is known for its relatively gentle terrain, some routes do offer more elevation gain for a moderate challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31992463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Bridge – Super road for racing bikes loop from La Puebla del Río&lt;/a&gt; includes over 350 meters of elevation gain, providing a more demanding experience for road cyclists.
tips: Beautiful lagoon located in the Dehesa de Abajo Concerted Natural Reserve, between the marshes of the Guadalquivir and rice fields of Isla Mayor. Perfect place to observe birds, for example flamingos.

Isla Mayor is a town located very close to the Doñana natural park, surrounded by rice fields. The week of June 24 the Rice and Crab Fair is celebrated. In the center you will find the modern parish church of San Rafael Arcángel.

It is a site that was formerly used as a garbage dump (an abandoned gravel pit), converted into a paradise for birds, in a protected wetland with high biodiversity. In 1991 the space was declared a Concerted Nature Reserve by the Ministry of the Environment.

waypoints.name: Views of the lagoon in the Dehesa de Abajo
